behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler wiring: raw body bytes + signature verifier + enqueue path. A unit test of HMAC math can pass while integration fails (e.g., body parsing/mutation before verify). Rejected higher level: behavior text only requires '401' and 'not forwarded'; it does not require real network/OS/subprocess, so System is not required. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Start the HTTP handler in-process with real middleware stack and a test queue adapter. Send a request with intentionally invalid X-Signature-256 over the in-process HTTP test client. Assert 401 and zero queue writes.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is a cross-module ordering invariant between HTTP handler and persistent queue commit/append. Unit tests of either module miss ordering bugs. Rejected System: behavior text specifies ordering with a persistent queue, not real network/subprocess behavior; can be validated with in-process server + local persistent backend.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process HTTP request plus real local persistent queue backend (e.g., sqlite/file-backed test instance). Instrument queue write completion and HTTP response completion; assert queue write finishes first, then 200 is sent.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at handoff boundary between request handler and async processor. A unit test can miss real await/Promise wiring mistakes. Rejected System: behavior text is about not awaiting downstream completion, which can be proven in-process without real network/OS effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with real async queue consumer stub that blocks on a controllable promise. Send request, assert response returns before unblocking consumer, and verify downstream eventually runs after response.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: This is a real-time SLO under concurrency ('within 3 seconds wall-clock' with slow concurrent work). In-process fakes or mocked clocks can hide event-loop saturation, socket backlog, and scheduler effects. No AI dependency, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service as a real process on a local port. Generate concurrent load with earlier events that trigger intentionally slow downstream work. Measure actual wall-clock latency of new webhook responses and assert p/threshold requirement: each target response <3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Duplicate suppression is a state handoff invariant across handler, dedupe store/index, and queue write. Unit logic checks may miss persistence/transaction ordering bugs. Rejected System: behavior text only requires dedupe correctness, not real network/OS primitives.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process handler with real local persistence for dedupe state/queue. Send same event ID twice (including near-concurrent requests). Assert exactly one persisted queue record and second request is handled without second write.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Behavior checks contract mapping between queue-full condition and HTTP response semantics (status + header). This is module interaction, not single-function logic. Rejected System: behavior text says 'queue reports it is full' and expected HTTP mapping; this can be reproduced in-process without real network/subprocess.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with queue implementation/config that can enter full state in test. Trigger full condition and submit request. Assert HTTP 503 and valid Retry-After header, and no enqueue side effects.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This validates queue module + persistence backend behavior under configured limits; unit tests with mocks can hide real persistence/counting edge cases. Rejected System: behavior text focuses on queue enforcement itself, not OS/network/subprocess interactions beyond local persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue backend in a test environment with small configured capacity. Enqueue up to limit, then enqueue one more. Assert overflow is rejected with the queue’s full error and persisted depth does not exceed configured capacity.
